home.title: A safe space that never knows your name
home.subtitle: Anonymous mental-health screening, support matching, and research participation — verified with your Socious Wallet, never your identity.
home.privacy: No phone. No real name. No tracking.
home.footer: Moya — open source (GPL-3.0), funded by Project Catalyst

auth.signupSubtitle: Pick a handle — your real name is never asked. Email is for sign-in and recovery only.
auth.handleHint: 3–30 characters: a–z, 0–9, dashes. This is your only name here.
auth.emailHint: Sign-in and recovery only. Never shown, never shared.
auth.noPhone: No phone. No real name — ever.

dashboard.title: Hello, {handle}
dashboard.verifyBannerBody: Prove one fact with your Socious Wallet: that you're over 18. Nothing else is shared.
dashboard.verifiedBanner: ✓ Verified — your eligibility credential is reusable everywhere in Moya.
dashboard.screeningBody: Nine questions clinicians use worldwide (PHQ-9). About two minutes.
dashboard.matchesBody: Ranked by your severity band, risk factors and preferences — never your identity.

verify.title: Verify with Socious Wallet
verify.demoNote: Demo mode: your account will verify on the next check.
verify.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
verify.cameraHint: 📷 Scan with your phone camera (not the wallet's scanner) — the page will hand off to Socious Wallet.
verify.orOpenLink: …or open this link on your phone (also sent by email)
verify.oneQrBody: One scan proves two facts at once: you completed identity verification in Socious Wallet, and you are over 18. Nothing else is shared with Moya.
verify.kycPrereq: Prerequisite: complete identity verification inside the Socious Wallet app first (Wallet → Verify). If you haven't, the wallet will offer it when you scan.

phq9.prompt: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
phq9.q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
phq9.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
phq9.privacyNote: Your answers are scored on our server and immediately discarded — only your total score and severity band are stored.

crisis.title: Before anything else —
crisis.body: You mentioned thoughts of self-harm. Whatever you are carrying right now, you deserve support today — and talking to someone can help.
crisis.label: Free, confidential, right now
crisis.line3: If you are in immediate danger, call 119.
crisis.note: Calling a hotline is anonymous and completely separate from this app.

screening.introBody: Nine questions (PHQ-9) that clinicians use worldwide. It takes about two minutes.
screening.introPrivacy: Your answers are never stored — only your severity band is used to match you with support.
screening.explain_minimal: Your answers suggest minimal symptoms right now. The check-in is always here when you need it.
screening.explain_mild: Your answers suggest mild symptoms. Many people in this range benefit from self-help support and keeping an eye on how they feel.
screening.explain_moderate: Scores of 10–14 suggest moderate symptoms. Many people in this range feel noticeably better with structured support — a conversation with a professional is a strong next step.
screening.explain_moderately_severe: Your answers suggest moderately severe symptoms. Talking to a professional soon is strongly recommended — support works.
screening.explain_severe: Your answers suggest severe symptoms. Please consider reaching out to a professional as soon as you can — you deserve support, and it helps.
screening.bandOnly: Only this band — never your answers — is used for matching.
screening.claimBody: Scan with Socious Wallet to store this result as a verifiable credential — it unlocks matched support and research participation without revealing your answers.

matches.title: Support matched to you
matches.rankedFor: Ranked for your {band} band and preferences.
matches.requested: ✓ Requested — the provider sees only your handle, band and preferences
matches.none: No matches yet — complete a check-in first.
matches.note: Matching uses your severity band, risk factors and preferences — never your identity.

ledger.title: Privacy Ledger
ledger.subtitle: Exactly what Moya knows about you — and what it never will.
ledger.knows_handle: Your handle and, once linked, your wallet's DID (pseudonymous)
ledger.knows_email: Your email — for sign-in and recovery only; never shown, never shared
ledger.knows_band: Your screening score and severity band — never your answers
ledger.knows_credentials: Which credentials you hold — as yes/no proofs only
ledger.never_documents: Your identity documents — they stay in your wallet, on your device
ledger.how: Verification happens in the Socious Wallet app on your device. Moya receives a yes/no proof — nothing else.
ledger.openSource: Every claim on this page is verifiable in our open-source code:

opps.title: Research & support opportunities
opps.subtitle: Participate anonymously. Rewards are paid from on-chain escrow to your reward address.
opps.note: Organizations see only your handle and proof flags — never your identity. Rewards release from an escrow smart contract on Cardano.

portal.title: Provider Portal
portal.anonNote: Patients appear as handles only — you never see names, contacts, or raw questionnaire answers.
portal.issueBody: Signs a verifiable credential to the patient's wallet. It contains only this attestation — no identity data, no session notes.
portal.claimHint: The patient scans this with Socious Wallet to claim (also sent by email).

console.title: Research Console
console.subtitle: Post studies, fund escrow on Cardano, release rewards on completion. Participants are handles — never identities.
console.note: Funding locks the reward at the escrow validator; release pays the participant from escrow. Both are public Cardano transactions.
